items.title: Protein-packed Cheerios and cat food: How General Mills plans to combat a tough spending backdrop
items.description: “Cat growth is on fire,” one executive said.

items.title: Opening a ‘Trump account’ for your children? Here is the risk you need to reckon with first.
items.description: The new accounts ban bonds and international stocks, forcing parents to bet their children’s financial future entirely on U.S. equities.

items.title: Nvidia is betting on a trillion-dollar robotics boom. Here is the hidden way to trade it.
items.description: CEO Jensen Huang has called humanoid robots a “multitrillion-dollar economic opportunity.”

items.title: ‘We were stunned’: My daughter, 39, said her mother-in-law gives her more money than we do. Do I call her out?
items.description: “She thanked us for the $100 birthday gift and then announced that her mother-in-law had sent her $400.”

items.title: CoreWeave, Nebius shares tumble as Meta stands to become a fresh threat in the cloud
items.description: Meta’s reported interest in monetizing its AI infrastructure is leading investors to question the sustainability of neocloud business models.

items.title: This is the best time ‘in a generation’ to buy space and defense stocks, analysts say
items.description: Wedbush analysts are taking a bullish view of the  space sector, initiating coverage of SpaceX and other stocks.

items.title: Where can I invest my kid’s ‘Trump account’ money? The Treasury Department just answered that question.
items.description: The money in “Trump accounts” has to be invested in low-cost index funds — and now parents and investors are learning which funds they can actually use.

items.title: ServiceNow and Salesforce shares now look like buys, as ‘Armageddon’ fears are too extreme, analyst says
items.description: A Guggenheim analyst says that valuations for the two software stocks are too depressed, even though the AI threat is real.

items.title: Sandisk’s and Micron’s stocks sink as the rotation trade builds, but supply shortages should limit losses
items.description: Most of Sandisk’s annual revenue could eventually come from its new business model contracts that provide better visibility, BofA said.

items.title: I’m 53 and want to retire in 12 years. Is 5% enough to put in my 401(k)?
items.description: If you want to have enough for retirement, you need to push your savings limits today.
